p.em: tcsetpgrp() designates which process group receives terminal signals

div.p: c shell_pgid = getpgrp(); // Shell's own PGID tcsetpgrp(STDIN, shell_pgid); // Take terminal control

ul.li: Ctrl+C → Terminal sends SIGINT to foreground PGID
Ctrl+Z → Terminal sends SIGTSTP to foreground PGID
All processes in the group receive the signal

div.p: The terminal doesn't send signals to individual processes — it sends to the entire foreground process group. This is why cmd1 | cmd2 | cmd3 all stop when you press Ctrl+Z.
